content.full_text:
State of California • Department of Corrections                PRISON INDUSTRY BOARD

George Deukmejian                          James Rowland, Chairman
Governor                                  Earlie A. Mays, Vice Chairman
                                         Members
           MAR 5 1990                     William E. Allen
                                         John S. Babich
                                         Robert E. Burton
March 2, 1990                            Leonard Greenstone
                                         Robert L. Hanna
                                         Samuel Hill, Jr.
                                         William E. Saracino
                                         Wayne A. Zitzke

                                         Carolyn G. Ortiz
                                         Executive Officer


Mr. Jeevan Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

     I would  like to request confirmation of our discussion regarding
advice on a possible conflict of interest on the part of a member
of the Prison Industry Board.  The Board is made up of appointees
of the Governor and the Legislature.  It sets policy for
and
oversees the operation of the Prison Industry Authority (PIA).

     As we discussed, one of the  Prison Industry Board members has
strongly supported the concept of the Prison Industry Authority
using a Manufacturers Representative firm to sell its products to
local governmental agencies.  PIA recently issued a Request for
Proposals and selected the firm of Electrical Supply of Salinas
to carry out this project.   Mr. Zitzke was not involved in the
selection of the firm.

     Because  of his interest in this area, however, Mr. Zitzke is
considering resigning from the Board in order to serve as a
consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas and market PIA
products to local governmental agencies.  We understand that if
there to do this, he would be prohibited from representing
Electrical Supply of Salinas before the Board and from assisting
them in formulating another contract with PIA.

     We also understand that there would be a conflict of interest if,
at the time of the contract, he knew or expected that he would
receive income from Electrical Supply of Salinas.

     It should be noted that the Board did not vote on either
establishing the overall program or selecting the individual


PRISON INDUSTRY BOARD              P.O. Box 942883  Sacramento, California  94283-0001
                                   1515 S Street, First Floor - South  •  (916) 445-2818

Mr. Jeevan Ahuja
Page 2


applicants.  The project was handled entirely by PIA staff.
Mr. Zitzke did make suggestions regarding implementation of the
project but not regarding any of the applicants.

I would appreciate receiving your written response confirming or
clarifying these issues as soon as possible.  Thank you for your
assistance.

Sincerely,

Carolyn G. Ortiz

CAROLYN G. ORTIZ
Executive Officer

cc:  Wayne Zitzke

sections.question: Whether a Prison Industry Board member (Mr. Zitzke) would have a conflict of interest if he resigned from the Board to serve as a consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas, which was recently selected by the Prison Industry Authority to sell its products to local governmental agencies. The request seeks confirmation regarding: (1) whether Mr. Zitzke would be prohibited from representing Electrical Supply before the Board or assisting in formulating another contract with PIA; and (2) whether there would be a conflict of interest if he knew or expected to receive income from Electrical Supply at the time of the contract.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a Prison Industry Board member violate conflict of interest laws by resigning to become a consultant for a firm that was selected by the Authority to represent its products, and what restrictions would apply to his future dealings with the Board?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a state agency seeking FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question is presented in narrative form within the body of the letter. The document lacks the typical structure of FPPC advice letters with explicitly marked Q/C sections.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a Prison Industry Board member (Mr. Zitzke) would have a conflict of interest if he resigned from the Board to serve as a consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas, which was recently selected by the Prison Industry Authority to sell its products to local governmental agencies. The request seeks confirmation regarding: (1) whether Mr. Zitzke would be prohibited from representing Electrical Supply before the Board or assisting in formulating another contract with PIA; and (2) whether there would be a conflict of interest if he knew or expected to receive income from Electrical Supply at the time of the contract.

Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Mr. Ahuja: I would like to request confirmation of our discussion regarding advice on a possible conflict of interest on the part of a member of the Prison Industry Board. The Board is made up of appointees of the Governor and the Legislature. It sets policy for and oversees the operation of the Prison Industry Authority (PIA). As we discussed, one of the Prison Industry Board members has strongly supported the concept of the Prison Industry Authority using a Manufacturers Representative firm to sell its products to local governmental agencies. PIA recently issued a Request for Proposals and selected the firm of Electrical Supply of Salinas to carry out this project. Mr. Zitzke was not involved in the selection of the firm. Because of his interest in this area, however, Mr. Zitzke is considering resigning from the Board in order to serve as a consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas and market PIA products to local governmental agencies. We understand that if there to do this, he would be prohibited from representing Electrical Supply of Salinas before the Board and from assisting them in formulating another contract with PIA. We also understand that there would be a conflict of interest if, at the time of the contract, he knew or expected that he would receive income from Electrical Supply of Salinas. It should be noted that the Board did not vote on either establishing the overall program or selecting the individual PRISON INDUSTRY BOARD P.O. Mr. Zitzke did make suggestions regarding implementation of the project but not regarding any of the applicants. I would appreciate receiving your written response confirming or clarifying these issues as soon as possible. Thank you for your assistance. Sincerely, Carolyn G. Ortiz CAROLYN G. embedding.summary: The Prison Industry Board's Executive Officer requests written FPPC confirmation regarding potential conflict of interest issues for board member Wayne Zitzke, who is considering resigning to serve as a consultant to Electrical Supply of Salinas, a firm recently selected by the Prison Industry Authority. The letter outlines the facts, including that Zitzke was not involved in selecting the firm and seeks clarification on prohibited activities and timing of the conflict.
